summary: Iter 223 — Phase B Readiness Report stub (GET /api/admin/workflow-observer/phase-b-readiness) verified end-to-end. Backend-only. All 9 pytest cases in tests/test_workflow_state_observer.py pass (5 pre-existing + 4 new readiness: not-ready-on-empty, categorizes-and-verdicts-ready, not-ready-below-threshold, clamps-min-coverage). Full 72/72 learning-core regression suite across 10 test files pass (test_workflow_state_observer, test_weekly_digest, test_u6_so_telemetry, test_unified_feedback, test_learning_core_events, test_pattern_health_core, test_drift_and_fingerprint, test_intake_learning_feedback, test_cold_start_matcher, test_vendor_profile_helpers). Authored new HTTP integration file tests/test_phase_b_readiness_http.py (15 tests, all green) against the live backend URL. Verified: (a) JSON schema has all required keys {window_days, since, total_calls, min_coverage, ready_to_extract, verdict, counts{must_preserve, should_cover, edge_case}, matrix, markdown}; (b) empty-data case → ready_to_extract=false, counts all zero, matrix=[], verdict contains 'NOT READY' and 'no observer data captured yet'; (c) ?format=markdown returns text/markdown content-type, body starts with '# Phase B Readiness Report', includes Verdict/Total calls sections; (d) parameter validation — format=foobar → 422, days=0/999 → 422, min_coverage=1/101 → 422; (e) populated-data case (seeded 10+3+1 via doc_id='readiness-test-iter223-*') correctly categorizes must_preserve=1 (10-calls, SALES_ORDER), should_cover=1 (3-calls, SHIPMENT), edge_case=1 (1-call, RECEIPT), matrix sorted desc by calls, verdict contains 'READY', markdown has all 3 section headers ('Must-preserve paths', 'Should-cover paths', 'Edge cases') with well-formed pipe-table rows; (f) below-threshold case (2 calls, min_coverage=5) → should_cover=1, ready_to_extract=false, verdict mentions 'min_coverage=5'; (g) regression endpoints /api/admin/workflow-observer/{summary,recent}, /api/learning/{digest/latest,pattern-health/unified}, /api/documents, /api/health all still 200. Seed data fully cleaned up (post-test count=0 for both readiness-test-iter223-* and readiness-seed-* prefixes). Giovanni C-10250, C-DEMO-OVRD-1, and 2026-W15 digest fixtures untouched.
critical_code_review_comments: services/workflow_state_observer.py grew from ~160 to ~295 lines with the new build_phase_b_readiness_report() + markdown renderer — still well under the 700-line threshold, still single-responsibility (observer + reporting on observer data). Good.

Readiness report rightly separates threshold config (min_coverage) from time window (days) and clamps both at the service layer (max(2, min(int, 100)) for min_coverage, via get_observer_summary for days). Router declares matching Query bounds, so HTTP-layer 422 and service-layer clamp agree on min_coverage (iter_222's flagged spec/impl mismatch on /summary days bounds is intentionally NOT mirrored here — good call).

Markdown pipe escape helper (_esc) is present and correct for both caller and doc_type. Verdict strings include the threshold value when not-ready-below-threshold, which makes the HTTP matrix output self-describing for PR reviewers.

Category thresholds are: ≥min_coverage → must_preserve, 2..min_coverage-1 → should_cover, 1 → edge_case. With default min_coverage=5, a 4-call path is 'should_cover' which matches the spec's narrative. One small observation (not a bug): if min_coverage is set to 2, there will be NO should_cover rows (the range 2..1 is empty) and everything with ≥2 goes to must_preserve, with 1 going to edge_case. That's mathematically correct but users choosing min_coverage=2 should know that's the degenerate split. Optional: add a brief docstring note.

routers/workflow_observer.py endpoint uses `format: str = Query('json', pattern='^(json|markdown)$')` — correctly returns 422 on foobar (FastAPI/pydantic v2 surfaces pattern mismatches as 422). Returns PlainTextResponse(text/markdown) on markdown path and dict (application/json) on json path. Clean.

No new env vars. No new deps. No MongoDB ObjectId leakage in either response (service layer uses {_id:0} projection). Phase B extraction target list is machine-readable (matrix[]) AND human-readable (markdown) in one call. Ship-worthy.
seed_data_creation: Inserted 14 temp docs (10+3+1 for populated test, + 2 for below-threshold test, separately) into workflow_state_observations with doc_id prefix 'readiness-test-iter223-'. Both test cases delete their own rows in a try/finally and the module-level fixture also runs a final delete_many({doc_id: {$regex: '^readiness-test-iter223-'}}). Verified post-test count=0 for readiness-test-iter223-* and readiness-seed-* prefixes. Giovanni C-10250, C-DEMO-OVRD-1, and 2026-W15 digest row untouched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 223 Phase B Readiness Report is verified green. To re-run the full iter 223 regression: `cd /app/backend && MONGO_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017 DB_NAME=gpi_document_hub REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://contract-intel-9.preview.emergentagent.com pytest tests/test_workflow_state_observer.py tests/test_phase_b_readiness_http.py tests/test_weekly_digest.py tests/test_u6_so_telemetry.py tests/test_unified_feedback.py tests/test_learning_core_events.py tests/test_pattern_health_core.py tests/test_drift_and_fingerprint.py tests/test_intake_learning_feedback.py tests/test_cold_start_matcher.py tests/test_vendor_profile_helpers.py --tb=short` → expects 87 passed (72 unit + 15 HTTP). The readiness endpoint is now the canonical gate for Phase B proper (427-line extraction) — when production observations accumulate, call GET /api/admin/workflow-observer/phase-b-readiness?days=7 and inspect `ready_to_extract` + `matrix[]` for the test-coverage target list.
rca of the issue: N/A — feature-addition verification pass, no defects found.
